metadata.name: intercom__admin_metrics
metadata.comment: Each record represents an individual admin (employee) and a unique team they are assigned on, enriched with admin specific conversation aggregates. For example, the admin's total conversations, average rating, and median response times by specific team.

admin_id.name: admin_id
admin_id.comment: The unique identifier representing the admin.

team_id.name: team_id
team_id.comment: The team ID the admin is associated with.

team_name.name: team_name
team_name.comment: The team the admin is associated with.

total_conversations_closed.name: total_conversations_closed
total_conversations_closed.comment: The admins sum of total closed conversations.

average_conversation_parts.name: average_conversation_parts
average_conversation_parts.comment: The admins average number of parts associated with a single conversation.

average_conversation_rating.name: average_conversation_rating
average_conversation_rating.comment: The admins total average rating given to conversations they closed.

median_conversations_reopened.name: median_conversations_reopened
median_conversations_reopened.comment: The admins median value of times they reopened conversations.

median_conversation_assignments.name: median_conversation_assignments
median_conversation_assignments.comment: The admins median value of times they closed a conversation and had other assignees on the conversation.

median_time_to_first_response_time_minut.name: median_time_to_first_response_time_minutes
median_time_to_first_response_time_minut.comment: The admins median value of time it took for their first response on a conversation.

median_time_to_last_close_minutes.name: median_time_to_last_close_minutes
median_time_to_last_close_minutes.comment: The admins median value of time it took to the last closure of a conversation.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: intercom__article_enhanced
metadata.comment: Each record represents a single help center article, enriched with data from collections, parent collections, authors (admins), and help centers. This model provides a denormalized view of articles with all related entity information for easier reporting and analysis on help center content performance.

article_id.name: article_id
article_id.comment: The unique identifier for the article.

author_id.name: author_id
author_id.comment: The unique identifier of the admin who authored the article.

author_name.name: author_name
author_name.comment: The name of the article author.

author_job_title.name: author_job_title
author_job_title.comment: The job title of the article author.

article_description.name: article_description
article_description.comment: The description or summary of the article.

article_body.name: article_body
article_body.comment: The full body content of the article (HTML).

article_url.name: article_url
article_url.comment: The public URL of the article.

article_state.name: article_state
article_state.comment: The publication state of the article (draft, published).

default_locale.name: default_locale
default_locale.comment: The default locale/language of the article.

statistics_conversations.name: statistics_conversations
statistics_conversations.comment: The number of conversations started from this article.

collection_id.name: collection_id
collection_id.comment: The unique identifier of the collection this article belongs to.

collection_name.name: collection_name
collection_name.comment: The name of the collection containing this article.

collection_url.name: collection_url
collection_url.comment: The public URL of the collection.

collection_icon.name: collection_icon
collection_icon.comment: The icon identifier for the collection.

collection_display_order.name: collection_display_order
collection_display_order.comment: The display order of the collection within its parent.

parent_collection_id.name: parent_collection_id
parent_collection_id.comment: The unique identifier of the parent collection (for nested sections).

parent_collection_name.name: parent_collection_name
parent_collection_name.comment: The name of the parent collection.

parent_collection_url.name: parent_collection_url
parent_collection_url.comment: The URL of the parent collection.

help_center_id.name: help_center_id
help_center_id.comment: The unique identifier of the help center.

is_in_nested_section.name: is_in_nested_section
is_in_nested_section.comment: Boolean flag indicating if the article is in a nested section (collection with parent).

help_center_name.name: help_center_name
help_center_name.comment: The name of the help center.

help_center_identifier.name: help_center_identifier
help_center_identifier.comment: The URL-friendly identifier for the help center.

is_help_center_website_enabled.name: is_help_center_website_enabled
is_help_center_website_enabled.comment: Boolean indicating whether the help center website is enabled.

article_created_at.name: article_created_at
article_created_at.comment: The time the article was created.

article_updated_at.name: article_updated_at
article_updated_at.comment: The time the article was last updated.

is_published.name: is_published
is_published.comment: Boolean flag indicating if article state is 'published'.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.name: _fivetran_start
_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran.

_fivetran_end.name: _fivetran_end
_fivetran_end.comment: Timestamp to record the time when a record became inactive in the source system.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: intercom__company_enhanced
metadata.comment: Each record represents a single company, enriched with data related to the company industry, monthly_spend, and user_count.

company_id.name: company_id
company_id.comment: The unique identifier representing the historical Intercom defined company.

website.name: website
website.comment: The website associated with the company.

industry.name: industry
industry.comment: The defined industry the company operates in.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time the company was created.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The time the company was last updated at.

user_count.name: user_count
user_count.comment: The total count of users associated with the company.

session_count.name: session_count
session_count.comment: The count of sessions the company has recorded.

monthly_spend.name: monthly_spend
monthly_spend.comment: How much revenue the company generates for your business.

plan_id.name: plan_id
plan_id.comment: Unique identifier representing the company plan.

plan_name.name: plan_name
plan_name.comment: The name of the plan you have associated with the company.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.name: _fivetran_start
_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran. This value may not be identical to the time the record was created in the source system.

_fivetran_end.name: _fivetran_end
_fivetran_end.comment: Timestamp to record the time when a record became inactive in the source system.

all_company_tags.name: all_company_tags
all_company_tags.comment: String aggregation of all tags associated with the company.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: intercom__company_metrics
metadata.comment: Each record represents a single row from intercom__company_enhanced, enriched with aggregates which determine the total conversation count, average satisfaction rating, median time to first response, and median time to last close with contacts associated to a single company.

company_id.name: company_id
company_id.comment: The unique identifier representing the historical Intercom defined company.

website.name: website
website.comment: The website associated with the company.

industry.name: industry
industry.comment: The defined industry the company operates in.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time the company was created.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The time the company was last updated at.

user_count.name: user_count
user_count.comment: The total count of users associated with the company.

session_count.name: session_count
session_count.comment: The count of sessions the company has recorded.

monthly_spend.name: monthly_spend
monthly_spend.comment: How much revenue the company generates for your business.

plan_id.name: plan_id
plan_id.comment: Unique identifier representing the company plan.

plan_name.name: plan_name
plan_name.comment: The name of the plan you have associated with the company.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.name: _fivetran_start
_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran. This value may not be identical to the time the record was created in the source system.

_fivetran_end.name: _fivetran_end
_fivetran_end.comment: Timestamp to record the time when a record became inactive in the source system.

all_company_tags.name: all_company_tags
all_company_tags.comment: String aggregation of all tags associated with the company.

total_conversations_closed.name: total_conversations_closed
total_conversations_closed.comment: The total closed conversations with contacts from that company.

average_conversation_parts.name: average_conversation_parts
average_conversation_parts.comment: The average number of parts associated to a single conversation with contacts from that company.

average_conversation_rating.name: average_conversation_rating
average_conversation_rating.comment: The average rating given to conversations from contacts associated with a single company.

median_conversations_reopened.name: median_conversations_reopened
median_conversations_reopened.comment: The median value of times a conversation was reopened with contacts from a single company.

median_time_to_first_response_time_minut.name: median_time_to_first_response_time_minutes
median_time_to_first_response_time_minut.comment: The median value of time it took for contacts of a single company to receive their first response on a conversation.

median_time_to_last_close_minutes.name: median_time_to_last_close_minutes
median_time_to_last_close_minutes.comment: The median value of time it took for contacts of a single company to receive their last closure of a conversation.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: intercom__contact_enhanced
metadata.comment: Each record represents a single contact, enriched with data identifying the contacts role, if they have unsubscribed from the email list, last contacted information, and which company they belong to.

contact_id.name: contact_id
contact_id.comment: The unique identifier for the contact which is given by Intercom.

admin_id.name: admin_id
admin_id.comment: The unique identifier representing the admin which has been assigned ownership of the contact.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time when the contact was created within Intercom.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The time when the contact was last updated.

signed_up_at.name: signed_up_at
signed_up_at.comment: The time the contact signed up.

contact_role.name: contact_role
contact_role.comment: The role of the contact (user or lead).

last_replied_at.name: last_replied_at
last_replied_at.comment: The time when the contact last messaged in.

last_email_clicked_at.name: last_email_clicked_at
last_email_clicked_at.comment: The time when the contact last clicked a link in an email.

last_email_opened_at.name: last_email_opened_at
last_email_opened_at.comment: The time when a contact last opened an email.

last_contacted_at.name: last_contacted_at
last_contacted_at.comment: The time when the contact was last messaged.

is_unsubscribed_from_emails.name: is_unsubscribed_from_emails
is_unsubscribed_from_emails.comment: Boolean indicating whether the contact has unsubscribed from emails.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.name: _fivetran_start
_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran. This value may not be identical to the time the record was created in the source system.

_fivetran_end.name: _fivetran_end
_fivetran_end.comment: Timestamp to record the time when a record became inactive in the source system.

all_contact_tags.name: all_contact_tags
all_contact_tags.comment: String aggregation of all tags associated with the contact.

all_contact_company_names.name: all_contact_company_names
all_contact_company_names.comment: String aggregation of all companies the contact is associated with.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: intercom__conversation_enhanced
metadata.comment: Each record represents a single conversation, enriched with data from the multiple conversation parts. The conversation parts provide relevant information such as who was assigned to the conversation, which contact the conversation was with, the current conversation state, who closed the conversation, and the final conversation ratings from the contact.

conversation_created_at.name: conversation_created_at
conversation_created_at.comment: The time the conversation was created.

conversation_last_updated_at.name: conversation_last_updated_at
conversation_last_updated_at.comment: The date the conversation was last updated at.

conversation_type.name: conversation_type
conversation_type.comment: The type of conversation. This includes conversation push facebook twitter and email.

conversation_initiated_type.name: conversation_initiated_type
conversation_initiated_type.comment: How the conversation was initially delivered to the responder.

conversation_subject.name: conversation_subject
conversation_subject.comment: The subject of the conversation created by the initiator.

conversation_assignee_id.name: conversation_assignee_id
conversation_assignee_id.comment: The ID of the user assigned to the conversation. If it is not assigned to a user it will return null.

conversation_assignee_type.name: conversation_assignee_type
conversation_assignee_type.comment: [DEPRECATED] The type of user the conversation is assigned to. If it is not assigned to a user it will show 'unassigned'. Use is_assignee_admin instead.

conversation_author_type.name: conversation_author_type
conversation_author_type.comment: The type of individual who authored the first message. Will be either contact or admin.

conversation_state.name: conversation_state
conversation_state.comment: The current state of the conversation (open or close).

is_read.name: is_read
is_read.comment: Boolean indicating whether the conversation message has been read.

waiting_since.name: waiting_since
waiting_since.comment: The last time a contact responded to an admin. The time a customer started waiting for a response. Set to null if the last reply is from an admin.

snoozed_until.name: snoozed_until
snoozed_until.comment: If set this is the time in the future when this conversation will be marked as open.

sla_name.name: sla_name
sla_name.comment: The name of the SLA as given by the teammate when it was created.

conversation_rating.name: conversation_rating
conversation_rating.comment: An optional field for the customer to rate the conversation which will be between 1 and 5.

conversation_remark.name: conversation_remark
conversation_remark.comment: An optional field to add a remark to correspond to the number rating.

is_assignee_admin.name: is_assignee_admin
is_assignee_admin.comment: Boolean indicating whether the conversation assignee is an admin user. Determined by joining assignee_id with the admin table. False if conversation is unassigned or assigned to a non-admin user.

first_close_at.name: first_close_at
first_close_at.comment: The time of the first conversation part where part_type was 'close' by any author type indicating the conversation was closed.

last_close_at.name: last_close_at
last_close_at.comment: The time of the last conversation part where part_type was 'close' by any author type indicating the conversation was closed.

first_admin_close_at.name: first_admin_close_at
first_admin_close_at.comment: The time of the first conversation part where part_type was 'close' by an admin indicating the conversation was closed.

last_admin_close_at.name: last_admin_close_at
last_admin_close_at.comment: The time of the last conversation part where part_type was 'close' by an admin indicating the conversation was closed.

all_conversation_tags.name: all_conversation_tags
all_conversation_tags.comment: String aggregation of all tags associated with the conversation.

first_close_by_admin_id.name: first_close_by_admin_id
first_close_by_admin_id.comment: The admin_id of the admin who was first assigned to the conversation.

last_close_by_admin_id.name: last_close_by_admin_id
last_close_by_admin_id.comment: The admin_id of the admin who last closed the conversation.

first_close_by_author_id.name: first_close_by_author_id
first_close_by_author_id.comment: The author_id of the author (of author_type admin, bot, user, etc.) who was first closed to the conversation.

last_close_by_author_id.name: last_close_by_author_id
last_close_by_author_id.comment: The author_id of the author (of author_type admin, bot, user, etc.) who was last closed to the conversation.

first_contact_author_id.name: first_contact_author_id
first_contact_author_id.comment: The contact_id of the contact who was first associated with the conversation.

last_contact_author_id.name: last_contact_author_id
last_contact_author_id.comment: The contact_id of the contact who was last associated with the conversation before closing.

first_team_id.name: first_team_id
first_team_id.comment: ID of the team that was first assigned to the conversation.

last_team_id.name: last_team_id
last_team_id.comment: ID of the team that was last assigned to the conversation.

first_team_name.name: first_team_name
first_team_name.comment: Name of the team that was first assigned to the conversation.

last_team_name.name: last_team_name
last_team_name.comment: Name of the team that was last assigned to the conversation.

all_contact_company_names.name: all_contact_company_names
all_contact_company_names.comment: String aggregation of all companies the first_author_contact is associated with.

all_conversation_admins.name: all_conversation_admins
all_conversation_admins.comment: String aggregate of all admins that contributed to the conversation.

all_conversation_contacts.name: all_conversation_contacts
all_conversation_contacts.comment: String aggregate of all contacts that contributed to the conversation.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: intercom__conversation_metrics
metadata.comment: Each record represents a single row from intercom__conversation_enhanced, enriched with aggregates which determine time to first response, time to first close, and time to last close.

conversation_created_at.name: conversation_created_at
conversation_created_at.comment: The time the conversation was created.

conversation_last_updated_at.name: conversation_last_updated_at
conversation_last_updated_at.comment: The date the conversation was last updated at.

conversation_type.name: conversation_type
conversation_type.comment: The type of conversation. This includes conversation push facebook twitter and email.

conversation_initiated_type.name: conversation_initiated_type
conversation_initiated_type.comment: How the conversation was initially delivered to the responder.

conversation_subject.name: conversation_subject
conversation_subject.comment: The subject of the conversation created by the initiator.

conversation_assignee_id.name: conversation_assignee_id
conversation_assignee_id.comment: The ID of the user assigned to the conversation. If it is not assigned to a user it will return null.

conversation_assignee_type.name: conversation_assignee_type
conversation_assignee_type.comment: [DEPRECATED] The type of user the conversation is assigned to. If it is not assigned to a user it will show 'unassigned'. Use is_assignee_admin instead.

conversation_author_type.name: conversation_author_type
conversation_author_type.comment: The type of individual who authored the first message. Will be either contact or admin.

conversation_state.name: conversation_state
conversation_state.comment: The current state of the conversation (open or close).

is_read.name: is_read
is_read.comment: Boolean indicating whether the conversation message has been read.

waiting_since.name: waiting_since
waiting_since.comment: The last time a contact responded to an admin. The time a customer started waiting for a response. Set to null if last reply is from an admin.

snoozed_until.name: snoozed_until
snoozed_until.comment: If set this is the time in the future when this conversation will be marked as open.

sla_name.name: sla_name
sla_name.comment: The name of the SLA as given by the teammate when it was created.

conversation_rating.name: conversation_rating
conversation_rating.comment: An optional field for the customer to rate the conversation which will be between 1 and 5.

conversation_remark.name: conversation_remark
conversation_remark.comment: An optional field to add a remark to correspond to the number rating.

is_assignee_admin.name: is_assignee_admin
is_assignee_admin.comment: Boolean indicating whether the conversation assignee is an admin user. Determined by joining assignee_id with the admin table. False if conversation is unassigned or assigned to a non-admin user.

first_close_at.name: first_close_at
first_close_at.comment: The time of the first conversation part where part_type was 'close' by any author type indicating the conversation was closed.

last_close_at.name: last_close_at
last_close_at.comment: The time of the last conversation part where part_type was 'close' by any author type indicating the conversation was closed.

first_admin_close_at.name: first_admin_close_at
first_admin_close_at.comment: The time of the first conversation part where part_type was 'close' by an admin indicating the conversation was closed.

last_admin_close_at.name: last_admin_close_at
last_admin_close_at.comment: The time of the last conversation part where part_type was 'close' by an admin indicating the conversation was closed.

all_conversation_tags.name: all_conversation_tags
all_conversation_tags.comment: String aggregation of all tags associated with the conversation.

first_close_by_admin_id.name: first_close_by_admin_id
first_close_by_admin_id.comment: The admin_id of the admin who was first assigned to the conversation.

last_close_by_admin_id.name: last_close_by_admin_id
last_close_by_admin_id.comment: The admin_id of the admin who last closed the conversation.

first_close_by_author_id.name: first_close_by_author_id
first_close_by_author_id.comment: The author_id of the author (of author_type admin, bot, user, etc.) who was first closed to the conversation.

last_close_by_author_id.name: last_close_by_author_id
last_close_by_author_id.comment: The author_id of the author (of author_type admin, bot, user, etc.) who was last closed to the conversation.

first_contact_author_id.name: first_contact_author_id
first_contact_author_id.comment: The contact_id of the contact who was first associated with the conversation.

last_contact_author_id.name: last_contact_author_id
last_contact_author_id.comment: The contact_id of the contact who was last associated with the conversation before closing.

all_contact_company_names.name: all_contact_company_names
all_contact_company_names.comment: String aggregation of all companies the first_author_contact is associated with.

all_conversation_admins.name: all_conversation_admins
all_conversation_admins.comment: String aggregate of all admins that contributed to the conversation.

all_conversation_contacts.name: all_conversation_contacts
all_conversation_contacts.comment: String aggregate of all contacts that contributed to the conversation.

count_reopens.name: count_reopens
count_reopens.comment: The count of conversation parts where the part_type value was 'open'. Note, a conversation part_type must be manually opened by an admin.

count_total_parts.name: count_total_parts
count_total_parts.comment: The count of total conversation parts for a single given conversation. Max parts are 500.

count_assignments.name: count_assignments
count_assignments.comment: The count of conversation parts where the part_type value contained 'assignment'.

first_contact_reply_at.name: first_contact_reply_at
first_contact_reply_at.comment: The time when the conversation author of type user or lead commented in a conversation.

first_assignment_at.name: first_assignment_at
first_assignment_at.comment: The time when the first conversation assignment was made.

time_to_first_assignment_minutes.name: time_to_first_assignment_minutes
time_to_first_assignment_minutes.comment: The time difference (not factoring in business hours) between the conversation creation and the first_assignment_at.

first_admin_response_at.name: first_admin_response_at
first_admin_response_at.comment: The time when the conversation part author of type admin first commented in a conversation.

time_to_first_response_minutes.name: time_to_first_response_minutes
time_to_first_response_minutes.comment: The time difference (not factoring in business hours) between the conversation creation and the first_admin_response_at.

time_to_admin_first_close_minutes.name: time_to_admin_first_close_minutes
time_to_admin_first_close_minutes.comment: The time difference (not factoring in business hours) between the first_contact_reply_at and the first_admin_close_at.

time_to_first_close_minutes.name: time_to_first_close_minutes
time_to_first_close_minutes.comment: The time difference (not factoring in business hours) between the first_contact_reply_at and the first_close_at.

first_reopen_at.name: first_reopen_at
first_reopen_at.comment: The time of the first conversation part where part_type was 'open' indicating the conversation was reopened.

last_assignment_at.name: last_assignment_at
last_assignment_at.comment: The time of the last conversation assignment.

time_to_last_assignment_minutes.name: time_to_last_assignment_minutes
time_to_last_assignment_minutes.comment: The time difference (not factoring in business hours) between the conversation creation and the last_assignment_at.

last_contact_reply_at.name: last_contact_reply_at
last_contact_reply_at.comment: The time of the last contact response within the conversation.

last_admin_response_at.name: last_admin_response_at
last_admin_response_at.comment: The time of the last admin response within the conversation.

last_reopen_at.name: last_reopen_at
last_reopen_at.comment: The time of the last conversation part where part_type was 'open' indicating the conversation was reopened.

time_to_admin_last_close_minutes.name: time_to_admin_last_close_minutes
time_to_admin_last_close_minutes.comment: The time difference (not factoring in business hours) between the last_admin_close_at and the first_contact_reply.

time_to_last_close_minutes.name: time_to_last_close_minutes
time_to_last_close_minutes.comment: The time difference (not factoring in business hours) between the last_close_at and the first_contact_reply.

metadata.name: stg_intercom__article_history
metadata.comment: Table containing historical information about help center articles within Intercom.

article_id.name: article_id
article_id.comment: The unique identifier for the article.

collection_id.name: collection_id
collection_id.comment: The unique identifier of the collection this article belongs to.

section_id.name: section_id
section_id.comment: The unique identifier of the section (nested collection) if applicable.

author_id.name: author_id
author_id.comment: The unique identifier of the admin who authored the article.

article_description.name: article_description
article_description.comment: The description or summary of the article.

article_body.name: article_body
article_body.comment: The full body content of the article (HTML).

article_url.name: article_url
article_url.comment: The public URL of the article.

article_state.name: article_state
article_state.comment: The publication state of the article (draft, published).

parent_type.name: parent_type
parent_type.comment: The type of parent container (collection, section).

default_locale.name: default_locale
default_locale.comment: The default locale/language of the article.

statistics_conversations.name: statistics_conversations
statistics_conversations.comment: The number of conversations started from this article.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time the article was created.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The time the article was last updated.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.name: _fivetran_start
_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran. This value may not be identical to the time the record was created in the source system.

_fivetran_end.name: _fivetran_end
_fivetran_end.comment: Timestamp to record the time when a record became inactive in the source system.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The time when a record was last updated by Fivetran.

contact_id.name: contact_id
contact_id.comment: The unique identifier for the contact which is given by Intercom.

admin_id.name: admin_id

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time when the contact was created within Intercom.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The time when the contact was last updated.

signed_up_at.name: signed_up_at
signed_up_at.comment: The time the contact signed up.

contact_role.name: contact_role
contact_role.comment: The role of the contact (will either be user or lead).

last_replied_at.name: last_replied_at
last_replied_at.comment: The time when the contact last messaged in.

last_email_clicked_at.name: last_email_clicked_at
last_email_clicked_at.comment: The time when the contact last clicked a link in an email.

last_email_opened_at.name: last_email_opened_at
last_email_opened_at.comment: The time when a contact last opened an email.

last_contacted_at.name: last_contacted_at
last_contacted_at.comment: The time when the contact was last messaged.

is_unsubscribed_from_emails.name: is_unsubscribed_from_emails
is_unsubscribed_from_emails.comment: Boolean indicating whether the contact has unsubscribed from emails.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran. This value may not be identical to the time the record was created in the source system.

metadata.name: stg_intercom__conversation_history
metadata.comment: Table containing the historical records of each conversation. Conversations are created when a contact replies to an outbound message, or when one admin directly sends a message to a single contact.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time the conversation was created.

assignee_id.name: assignee_id
assignee_id.comment: The id of the user assigned to the conversation. If it is not assigned to a user it will return null.

assignee_type.name: assignee_type
assignee_type.comment: [DEPRECATED] The type of user the conversation is assigned to. If it is not assigned to a user it will return null. Use is_assignee_admin in the conversation_enhanced model instead.

conversation_rating_value.name: conversation_rating_value
conversation_rating_value.comment: An optional field for the customer to rate the conversation which will be between 1 and 5.

conversation_rating_remark.name: conversation_rating_remark
conversation_rating_remark.comment: An optional field to add a remark to correspond to the number rating.

first_contact_reply_created_at.name: first_contact_reply_created_at
first_contact_reply_created_at.comment: Time of the first text conversation part from a contact.

first_contact_reply_type.name: first_contact_reply_type
first_contact_reply_type.comment: The type of communication in which the first contact reply was.

is_read.name: is_read
is_read.comment: Boolean indicating whether the conversation message has been read.

source_author_id.name: source_author_id
source_author_id.comment: id of the individual who authored the first message.

source_author_type.name: source_author_type
source_author_type.comment: The type of individual who authored the first message.

source_delivered_as.name: source_delivered_as
source_delivered_as.comment: How the conversation was initially delivered to the responder.

source_type.name: source_type
source_type.comment: The type of conversation. This includes conversation, push, facebook, twitter and email.

source_subject.name: source_subject
source_subject.comment: The subject of the conversation created by the initiator.

state.name: state
state.comment: The current state of the conversation (open or close).

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The date the conversation was last updated at.

waiting_since.name: waiting_since
waiting_since.comment: The last time a contact responded to an admin. The time a customer started waiting for a response. Set to null if the last reply is from an admin.

snoozed_until.name: snoozed_until
snoozed_until.comment: If set, this is the time in the future when this conversation will be marked as open.

sla_name.name: sla_name
sla_name.comment: The name of the SLA as given by the teammate when it was created.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.name: _fivetran_start
_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran. This value may not be identical to the time the record was created in the source system.

_fivetran_end.name: _fivetran_end
_fivetran_end.comment: Timestamp to record the time when a record became inactive in the source system.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_intercom__conversation_part_history
metadata.comment: Table containing data related to the individual parts of a single conversation.

conversation_part_id.name: conversation_part_id
conversation_part_id.comment: The id representing the conversation part.

body.name: body
body.comment: Optional field that contains the body of the conversation part. Typically only populated for comment and assignment part_types.

conversation_id.name: conversation_id
conversation_id.comment: The id representing the parent conversation.

author_id.name: author_id
author_id.comment: The id representing the individual that sent the message.

author_type.name: author_type
author_type.comment: The type of individual that sent the message (user, admin, or bot).

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time the conversation part was created.

assigned_to_id.name: assigned_to_id
assigned_to_id.comment: The id of the admin that was assigned the conversation by this conversation_part (null if there has been no change in assignment).

assigned_to_type.name: assigned_to_type
assigned_to_type.comment: The type of the individual which the conversation part is assigned.

conversation_updated_at.name: conversation_updated_at
conversation_updated_at.comment: The time the parent conversation was last updated at.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The time the conversation part was last updated at.

_fivetran_active.name: _fivetran_active
_fivetran_active.comment: Boolean to identify an active record.

_fivetran_start.name: _fivetran_start
_fivetran_start.comment: Timestamp to record the time that a record is ingested by Fivetran. This value may not be identical to the time the record was created in the source system.

_fivetran_end.name: _fivetran_end
_fivetran_end.comment: Timestamp to record the time when a record became inactive in the source system.
